speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Where is he?

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: I did not see him since.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: See where he is, who’s with him, what he does.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I did not send you. If you find him sad,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Say I am dancing; if in mirth, report

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That I am sudden sick. Quick, and return.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Madam, methinks, if you did love him dearly,

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: You do not hold the method to enforce

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: The like from him.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What should I do I do not?

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: In each thing give him way; cross him in nothing.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Thou teachest like a fool: the way to lose him.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Tempt him not so too far. I wish, forbear.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: In time we hate that which we often fear.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: But here comes Antony.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I am sick and sullen.

speaker: ANTONY
text: I am sorry to give breathing to my purpose—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Help me away, dear Charmian! I shall fall.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: It cannot be thus long; the sides of nature

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Will not sustain it.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Now, my dearest queen—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Pray you stand farther from me.

speaker: ANTONY
text: What’s the matter?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I know by that same eye there’s some good news.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What, says the married woman you may go?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Would she had never given you leave to come.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Let her not say ’tis I that keep you here.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I have no power upon you. Hers you are.

speaker: ANTONY
text: The gods best know—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O, never was there queen

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: So mightily betrayed! Yet at the first

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I saw the treasons planted.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Cleopatra—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Why should I think you can be mine, and true—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Though you in swearing shake the thronèd gods—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Who have been false to Fulvia? Riotous madness,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To be entangled with those mouth-made vows

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Which break themselves in swearing!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Most sweet queen—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Nay, pray you seek no color for your going,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: But bid farewell and go. When you sued staying,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Then was the time for words. No going then!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Eternity was in our lips and eyes,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Bliss in our brows’ bent; none our parts so poor

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: But was a race of heaven. They are so still,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Or thou, the greatest soldier of the world,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Art turned the greatest liar.

speaker: ANTONY
text: How now, lady?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I would I had thy inches. Thou shouldst know

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: There were a heart in Egypt.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Hear me, queen:

speaker: ANTONY
text: The strong necessity of time commands

speaker: ANTONY
text: Our services awhile, but my full heart

speaker: ANTONY
text: Remains in use with you. Our Italy

speaker: ANTONY
text: Shines o’er with civil swords; Sextus Pompeius

speaker: ANTONY
text: Makes his approaches to the port of Rome;

speaker: ANTONY
text: Equality of two domestic powers

speaker: ANTONY
text: Breed scrupulous faction; the hated grown to strength

speaker: ANTONY
text: Are newly grown to love; the condemned Pompey,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Rich in his father’s honor, creeps apace

speaker: ANTONY
text: Into the hearts of such as have not thrived

speaker: ANTONY
text: Upon the present state, whose numbers threaten;

speaker: ANTONY
text: And quietness, grown sick of rest, would purge

speaker: ANTONY
text: By any desperate change. My more particular,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And that which most with you should safe my going,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Is Fulvia’s death.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Though age from folly could not give me freedom,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: It does from childishness. Can Fulvia die?

speaker: ANTONY
text: She’s dead, my queen.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Look here, and at thy sovereign leisure read

speaker: ANTONY
text: The garboils she awaked; at the last, best,

speaker: ANTONY
text: See when and where she died.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O, most false love!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Where be the sacred vials thou shouldst fill

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: With sorrowful water? Now I see, I see,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: In Fulvia’s death, how mine received shall be.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Quarrel no more, but be prepared to know

speaker: ANTONY
text: The purposes I bear, which are or cease

speaker: ANTONY
text: As you shall give th’ advice. By the fire

speaker: ANTONY
text: That quickens Nilus’ slime, I go from hence

speaker: ANTONY
text: Thy soldier, servant, making peace or war

speaker: ANTONY
text: As thou affects.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Cut my lace, Charmian, come!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: But let it be; I am quickly ill and well;

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: So Antony loves.

speaker: ANTONY
text: My precious queen, forbear,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And give true evidence to his love, which stands

speaker: ANTONY
text: An honorable trial.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: So Fulvia told me.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I prithee turn aside and weep for her,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Then bid adieu to me, and say the tears

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Belong to Egypt. Good now, play one scene

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Of excellent dissembling, and let it look

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Like perfect honor.

speaker: ANTONY
text: You’ll heat my blood. No more!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: You can do better yet, but this is meetly.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Now by my sword—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And target. Still he mends.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: But this is not the best. Look, prithee, Charmian,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: How this Herculean Roman does become

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The carriage of his chafe.

speaker: ANTONY
text: I’ll leave you, lady.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Courteous lord, one word.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Sir, you and I must part, but that’s not it;

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Sir, you and I have loved, but there’s not it;

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That you know well. Something it is I would—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O, my oblivion is a very Antony,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And I am all forgotten.

speaker: ANTONY
text: But that your Royalty

speaker: ANTONY
text: Holds idleness your subject, I should take you

speaker: ANTONY
text: For idleness itself.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: ’Tis sweating labor

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To bear such idleness so near the heart

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: As Cleopatra this. But, sir, forgive me,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Since my becomings kill me when they do not

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Eye well to you. Your honor calls you hence;

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Therefore be deaf to my unpitied folly,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And all the gods go with you. Upon your sword

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Sit laurel victory, and smooth success

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Be strewed before your feet.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Let us go. Come.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Our separation so abides and flies

speaker: ANTONY
text: That thou, residing here, goes yet with me,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And I, hence fleeting, here remain with thee.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Away!
